title: Lord of the Rings The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J R R Tolkien
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Lord of the Rings The Fellowship of the Ring" book by J R R Tolkien?
Options:
A. He complimented her appearance and said he would briefly recount the events without asking Elrond, but urged her to sleep again afterwards. He explained that the Riders directly pursued her when she ran off. They no longer required their horses' guidance, as she was visible to them, being so close to their world. Also, the Ring attracted them. Her friends leaped off the road or they would have been trampled. They knew nothing could save her if the white horse failed. The Riders were too swift to outpace and too numerous for even Glorfindel and Aragorn to withstand together.
B. He praised her looks and said he'd tell a short version of the story without consulting Elrond first, but she must sleep again after. When she fled, the Riders went right for her. They didn't need the horses anymore to guide them - she was visible to them, being on the edge of their world. Also, the Ring drew them. Her friends jumped off the road or they would've been run down. They knew nothing could save her if the white horse couldn't. The Riders were too fast to outrun and too many for even Glorfindel and Aragorn to stand against together.
C. ‘You look splendid,’ he said aloud. ‘I will risk a brief tale without consulting Elrond. But quite brief, mind you, and then you must sleep again. This is what happened, as far as I can gather. The Riders made straight for you, as soon as you fled. They did not need the guidance of their horses any longer: you had become visible to them, being already on the threshold of their world. And also the Ring drew them. Your friends sprang aside, off the road, or they would have been ridden down. They knew that nothing could save you, if the white horse could not. The Riders were too swift to overtake, and too many to oppose. On foot even Glorfindel and Aragorn together could not withstand all the Nine at once.
D. He complimented her appearance and said he would briefly tell the story without asking permission from Elrond, but that it must be quick and then she should rest again. He explained that the Riders went straight for her as soon as she ran away. The horses were no longer needed to guide them, since she was visible to the Riders, being so close to their world. Also, the Ring attracted them. Her friends jumped off the road so they would not be trampled. They knew nothing could save her if the white horse failed. The Riders were too fast to outrun and too many for even Glorfindel and Aragorn to face together. 
Answer:

options.A: He complimented her appearance and said he would briefly recount the events without asking Elrond, but urged her to sleep again afterwards. He explained that the Riders directly pursued her when she ran off. They no longer required their horses' guidance, as she was visible to them, being so close to their world. Also, the Ring attracted them. Her friends leaped off the road or they would have been trampled. They knew nothing could save her if the white horse failed. The Riders were too swift to outpace and too numerous for even Glorfindel and Aragorn to withstand together.
options.B: He praised her looks and said he'd tell a short version of the story without consulting Elrond first, but she must sleep again after. When she fled, the Riders went right for her. They didn't need the horses anymore to guide them - she was visible to them, being on the edge of their world. Also, the Ring drew them. Her friends jumped off the road or they would've been run down. They knew nothing could save her if the white horse couldn't. The Riders were too fast to outrun and too many for even Glorfindel and Aragorn to stand against together.
options.C: ‘You look splendid,’ he said aloud. ‘I will risk a brief tale without consulting Elrond. But quite brief, mind you, and then you must sleep again. This is what happened, as far as I can gather. The Riders made straight for you, as soon as you fled. They did not need the guidance of their horses any longer: you had become visible to them, being already on the threshold of their world. And also the Ring drew them. Your friends sprang aside, off the road, or they would have been ridden down. They knew that nothing could save you, if the white horse could not. The Riders were too swift to overtake, and too many to oppose. On foot even Glorfindel and Aragorn together could not withstand all the Nine at once.
options.D: He complimented her appearance and said he would briefly tell the story without asking permission from Elrond, but that it must be quick and then she should rest again. He explained that the Riders went straight for her as soon as she ran away. The horses were no longer needed to guide them, since she was visible to the Riders, being so close to their world. Also, the Ring attracted them. Her friends jumped off the road so they would not be trampled. They knew nothing could save her if the white horse failed. The Riders were too fast to outrun and too many for even Glorfindel and Aragorn to face together.
